rows.description: Soluta varia.
rows.notes: Page title clearly written at top centre; no amounts associated and very legible.

rows.description: Account Dinner
rows.notes: Clear structural label introducing the account/dinner items; no amount in the right column immediately opposite the words.

rows.description: 4 money baggs and Files
rows.notes: Description legible but the small numerals at far right on this line are faint/partly cut; could not reliably assign pounds/shillings/pence so left amounts blank.

rows.description: To Coquys for fewel &c
rows.notes: Entry text readable; numerals in the columns are blurred at this row and ambiguous, so amounts omitted rather than risk misalignment.

rows.description: To Collison Carpenter from Febr. 16. to Novr. 8th 99
rows.notes: Description clear (dates legible). The numeric amount to the right is larger than surrounding rows but partially obscured by ink and the page curl; I did not assign a numeric value.

rows.description: To West. Bookbinder for mr Wells his mapps
rows.notes: Description legible; the right-hand numerals are faint but a small value seems present — I left amounts blank because exact digits are unclear.

rows.description: To Eldridge for 29 Lnd. of rubble, 3 Lnd of gravel and 1 of mould
rows.notes: Description readable though cramped; the amount column is smudged here and individual £/s/d digits could not be separated reliably.

rows.description: To James Hilliard Glazier
rows.notes: Name and role (glazier) clear; amount area shows small numbers but partially obscured by vertical crease — left blank to avoid misreading.

rows.description: To mr Jerman on his restoration to ye profits of his fellowship past by ye visitors order
rows.notes: The numeric grouping '3 14 9' at the right is well aligned with the three amount columns and digits are distinct; shillings 14 and pence 9 are within valid ranges.

rows.description: Lost by ye fall of Guiniea put into ye chest 1698
rows.notes: Description legible but the amount to the right is very faint (ink faded) and potentially overwritten; I could not read a reliable £/s/d.

rows.description: To Boyer for for work in ye back quadrangle
rows.notes: Entry is readable; numerals are present but blurred and partly cut off by the page edge, so amounts are left blank.

rows.description: To mr Rothwell a minister by consent
rows.notes: Text clear; small numeric group at right is faint and ambiguous — better to leave empty than misassign digits.

rows.description: Laid out by mr Hutchins for ye Commons library
rows.notes: Description legible. Amount column at this line is faint; cannot reliably resolve £/s/d digits due to ink blots.

rows.description: To Coquys for Chym, New years day, and Epiphany dinners
rows.notes: Numeric '0 12 6' is clearly visible and well aligned to the amount columns; shillings 12 and pence 6 are legible.

rows.description: For stampt paper and a citation &c Paynter
rows.notes: Description partly contracted but readable. The right-hand numerals here are small and smudged; could not determine a reliable amount.

rows.description: For 3 horses and charges in going to Hanborow
rows.notes: Handwriting of description clear; amount figures on right are faint and partially overlapped by adjacent marks, so omitted.

rows.description: To Ralph Cox for buffes and mats for ye chapell (more to him)
rows.notes: Description legible; a two-digit shilling value seems present in the amount column but is not distinct enough to transcribe confidently.

rows.description: To Day ye Smith March 15th
rows.notes: Entry and date clear; numeric amount faint and partially obscured by shading — left blank for accuracy.

rows.description: For two money baggs
rows.notes: Text readable but amount column faint and ambiguous; no reliable digits could be read.

rows.description: (Candles)
rows.notes: A labelled bracket 'Candles' (section heading) is clearly written; no amount shown on that line itself.

rows.description: To Cosers for ye chapell
rows.notes: The right column shows '0 7 7' with a small loop after the pence that resembles the halfpenny mark (interpreted as 0.5). The fraction mark is faint but consistent with 'ob' halfpenny style.

rows.description: To Broadwater for ye Buttery for a year
rows.notes: Numeric '0 4 11' is relatively clear and aligns with the amount columns; digits distinct and within valid ranges.

rows.description: To Pain ye Brazier
rows.notes: Right-hand digits align to '0 5 6' fairly clearly; slight ink spread but digits interpretable and valid.

rows.description: To Gipfield ye Slater
rows.notes: Shillings and pence look like '11 6' though the leading pounds column is a small '00'; overall readable but with some blotting around the '11'.

rows.description: Taxes allowed to George Jennings
rows.notes: This is a grouped line with two names (George / Jennings) and several numeric annotations in the margin referring to sums; the numbers are written over and abbreviated ('47 6?' etc.) and I could not disambiguate exact £/s/d per name, so left amounts blank.

rows.description: To James Davis Joiner
rows.notes: A small '0 2 0' (or '0 2 2' — pence digit faint) appears opposite this entry. I recorded 0s 2s 0d as a conservative reading; the pence digit is not perfectly distinct which lowers confidence.

rows.description: For 3 horses going to Longwitham and charges for revising ye chancell
rows.notes: Long line of text is clear, but the right-hand numeric is very faint and partly obscured by a horizontal crease — no reliable amount could be read.

rows.description: To Jo Halifax Ironmonger for Nails &c abt ye house of Office
rows.notes: A clear leading '3' in the pounds column is visible, with following '1' and '2' in shillings and pence respectively. Ink is somewhat darker here; digits align well to columns.

rows.description: To Townsend ye mason from Apr. 29. 99. to May 31. 1701
rows.notes: Numeric group '5 9 4' is visible though the pence digit is slightly cramped; column alignment and digit shapes indicate 5£ 9s 4d.

rows.description: To Mrs Edwards towards ye rebuilding of ye south end of ye vicarage house of Kidlington by consent
rows.notes: Marginal notation '0:2:10' is clearly written at the left of the entry (visible as '0.2.10') and aligns with the amount columns; digits are distinct.

rows.description: For Buttery books at Nat. and Bapt.
rows.notes: A larger figure appears for this line. I read the left-most amount as '30' (pounds) with subsequent '1' shilling and '4' pence, but the tens digit is slightly unusual for the page and the surrounding ink makes the leading digit uncertain.

rows.description: To Mrs Bugge by consent
rows.notes: Right-hand cluster reads like '0 1 6' with decent alignment; ink is lighter but digits are separable.

rows.description: For mr Rensell, mine and my many journys to Oxfield abt ye & other ffarms given ye coll. in reversion
rows.notes: Text is long but readable; amount area shows '0 3 1' followed by a tiny flourish that could be a farthing or accidental ink (I interpreted that flourish as 3 farthings = 0.75). This is uncertain because the fractional mark is ambiguous.

rows.description: For 4 horses 6 days for ye journey
rows.notes: Right column appears as '0 1 10' — pence '10' is within 0–11 and the three columns align, though the pence digit is slightly crowded.

rows.description: For weeding and cleansing ye buryard
rows.notes: Small amount visible as '0 0 9' or similar; pence '9' readable though pounds/shillings are clearly zero.

rows.description: To mr Clark bookseller for cutting books, paper &c
rows.notes: Right-hand reads '0 12 0' fairly clearly; digits are darker here and alignment is consistent with the amount columns.

rows.description: For 10 qts of Red Galicia Oct. 5th 1701
rows.notes: I read '0 12 6' though the final digit is somewhat cramped and there is minor ink bleed; values are within valid ranges.

rows.description: To Will: Day, Smith Novembr. 1st 1701
rows.notes: Small amount visible but partly cut by the shadow at the bottom of the page; pence '12' is at the maximum allowed (12 pence would usually convert to 1 shilling) — the reading could be 0s 11d or 1s 0d; I recorded 0:0:12 but confidence is low due to uncertainty and the awkward placement.

rows.description: To James Helliard Glazier
rows.notes: Another glazier line at the bottom shows small '0 0 3' with a faint halfpenny loop after the pence; the fractional mark is faint hence moderate confidence.

rows.description: To Collison carpenter
rows.notes: Final listed name has a numeric cluster resembling '0 3 10' with a trailing faint small mark that could be a farthing (0.25). The farthing mark is ambiguous and the pence digit might alternatively be '9'; low confidence.

rows.description: 32j:16:6 (final sum shown in ledger)
rows.notes: Bottom-left total reads as '32:16:6' (the middle character looks like a 'j' stylistic but represents :, i.e. 32 pounds 16 shillings 6 pence). Some ink smudging reduces confidence slightly.

rows.description: 34:10:10 (vertical marginal total at lower right)
rows.notes: A secondary total on the far right bottom appears as '34:10:10' but the digits are cramped and partly cut off by the page edge; recorded with low confidence.
